calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-05-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gordon Oliver, for and with petitioner Debra Johnson.

The Court expressed deep concerns regarding actions taken without court authorization by the personal representative during the administration of the estate, including misuse of estate funds, improper distributions, and the failure to accont for expenditures. He inquired why the personal representative should not be surcharged for these improper actions. Mr. Oliver stated that Ms. Johnson's siblings had all accepted the actions of Ms. Johnson and none of them either objected or requested a surcharge. He stated that for most of the estate administration Ms. Johnson was acting without the benefit of legal counsel to advise her. He stated that she was acting in good faith, and that she believed she was acting under powers granted to her under Independent Administration of Estate authorization. Ms. Johnson stated that she was not intentionally abusing the estate funds, but that when a family member was in need she gave that what was needed. She apologized to the Court, and said that it was due to her misunderstanding of her powers. She stated that her two attorneys prior to Mr. Oliver had expired while representing her, and that she had contacted eight or nine other attorneys to assume the representation, buth that they either did not want to step into the mess the estate was in, or demanded a retainer which was too high for the family to afford. The Court inquired about the requested $5000 reserve fund and its anticipated use. Ms. Johnson replied that it was for unpaid attorneys fees, and that there were no other anticipated expenditures. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Continued To Aug-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Continued To Jul-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gordon Oliver for and with (executor) Debra Johnson.

The Court stated that it has concerns, that there is $300,000.00 that is unaccounted for, that distributions have not been equal, that there are disbursements for gambling trips, personal expenses and some that are not accounted for, confirmed that Ladoris was the decedent's ex-wife and stated that she assigned her interest to others, that the will said not to sell the property but it was sold, that we only have a declaration from the attorney, that the estate was illegally and badly handled and that now there is money that is gone. Ms. Johnson stated that $150,000.00 went to her aunt, that she forgot to inform Mr. Oliver of this, that there was additional property sold which brought up the balance and that her aunt was a 50% owner in the joint tenancy property in Texas. The Court asked if the $936,000.00 in gross proceeds was of this estate or the Texas estate, that Ms. Johnson has not observed any of the legal formalities, that you cannot transfer funds between estates, that you cannot pay Costco bill and that it is trying to understand just the money from this estate. Ms. Johnson stated that while she was dealing with the Texas property she was informed by the real estate investors there that she had to do an ancillary probate in Texas. Mr. Oliver stated that he does not know about the Texas property. The Court asked about the $300,000.00 that is missing. Mr. Oliver stated that they may be able to get closer than they are. The Court stated that it has information from the checking account and that much of it is not identified, that it will give Ms. Johnson one more chance, that it is considering imposing a large surcharge to reflect everything that has been done wrong including the missing money not accounted for and that efforts made in the accounting show that the administrator used money for personal matters. The accounting petition was continued to July 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Continued To May-31-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Continued To Apr-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gordon Oliver for and with Debra Johnson.

The Court stated that we now have a receipt from Brian, that we only have receipts totaling $410,000.00 from the $936,000.00 from the sale of the property, that the will prohibited the sale of the property, asked where the remaining proceeds are and asked why $100,000.00 was distributed to Debra and only $60,000.00 to the other beneficiaries. Mr. Oliver confirmed that the will says that the property can never be sold. The Court stated that a petition for instructions should have been filed and that there may have been a good to sell. Ms. Johnson stated that she had two attorney's who died, that she now has a third attorney, that there was an ancillary proceeding in Texas on the properties there, that the will said those properties could not be sold but the court voided it out and that she was not aware the same needed to be done here. The Court stated that someone ruled in Texas but not here and that money was distributed without court approval. Ms. Johnson responded that she thought she could distribute, that the other money from the sale was used to pay back property taxes on all of the properties and to clean out the garbage and trash in the San Francisco property, that they are still paying property taxes in Oakland and Texas, that she did not knowingly do anything illegal and that she received more than her siblings for administering the estate and reimbursement of filing fees. The Court stated that you need to apply and get approval for fees as well as costs and that you cannot just take a bigger chunk of the inheritance. Ms. Johnson stated that the powers of the independent administration mean nothing. The Court replied that it means a lot, that she should talk to her attorney regarding it and explained what it covers. Ms. Johnson stated that everything has been discussed with her siblings and that there are no objections. Mr. Oliver stated that Ms. Johnson did not understand. The Court stated that she signed the Duties & Liabilities which explains the responsibilities. Mr. Oliver stated that they will do whatever the court wants. The Court stated that it does not think the property should have been sold but it has happened, that there were no objections, that we should have a reconciliation of the sale price and amounts distributed and that the unequal distributions have to be addressed. The Court stated that it will continue the matter and that if they end up requesting fees they will need to file an amendment not a supplement and that an amendment will need to be noticed. The accounting petition was continued to April 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Debra D. Johnson, Petitioner in pro per.
The matter was duly called for hearing at 9:10 am. No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. The Court set an Order to Show Cause re Removal of Representative hearing for July 29, 2015.
While the judicial officer was still on the bench, Ms. Johnson arrived late (after 10:00 am). The case was recalled, and the Court directed that the July 29, 2015 Order to Show Cause hearing and Order to Appear be taken off calendar. The Court expressed concerns regarding the ability of Ms. Johnson to proceed without professional legal representation. Ms. Johnson responded that she was being assisted by a probate paralegal, and that she would be filing some waiver of notice forms later today. ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-17-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Mar-17-2015 Continued To Apr-28-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathon Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

The court stated that this hearing was specially set at 10:30 a.m. at the request of Ms. Johnson who has not appeared. Mr. Ferdon explained to the court the reasons the SFPA would not be inclined to step in and administer this estate, what he found out when reviewing the file and suggested the court contact the siblings of Ms. Johnson. The court stated that it does not intend to appoint the SFPA and will pursue the matter with Ms. Johnson. Mr. Ferdon stated that if the SFPA was to be appointed the court would need to tell them exactly what they would be taking possession of. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Debra Johnson to appear on April 7, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Ms. Johnson arrived in court at 11:50 a.m. and the matter was re-called. Ms. Johnson reported that she has interviewed with two attorneys but is unable to retain them due to costs, that she has spoken with a document preparer who was unable to help her and that she will be meeting with her siblings to see if any of them know of an attorney that will help. The court provided Ms. Johnson with a list of Probate Paralegals and suggested she also contact the Bar Association of Contra Costa County as they should have a panel of attorneys that will work Pro Bono or for a reduced rate. Ms. Johnson requested a 5-6 week continuance. The status of filing petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration was continued to April 28, 2015 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear will not be sent. The clerk verified Ms. Johnson's current address and Ms. Johnson agreed to file a change of address with the court today.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Continued To Oct-01-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Debra Johnson, Propria Persona.

Ms. Johnson spoke about the condition of the property, stated that their were squatters on the property and that they were in fear of fire due to stored flammables. Ms. Johnson also stated that the house was sold there was almost $300,000.00 that fees, fines, abatement and taxes were paid, the taxes were paid on the out of state property, that the Alameda property is occupied by a family member and that money was distributed to the heirs. The court pointed out that the property was distributed without court order. Ms. Johnson further explained that there are a total of eight heirs and asked for something from this court stating that the portion/language of the will regarding the Texas property is invalid. The court stated that it would have the court examiner contact Ms. Johnson and advised her that a petition for final distribution on waiver of account or a petition for interpretation of the will is needed. Ms. Johnson provided the court with her contact information. The status on filing of the petition for final distribution was continued to October 1, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
